articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Judicial Reforms: Focus on Efficiency and Accountability
articles.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted its report, urging comprehensive reforms in the Indian judiciary.
• Key recommendations include setting up specialized commercial courts, increasing judicial appointments, and improving infrastructure.
• The report emphasizes leveraging AI and digital tools for case management and reducing pendency.
• It also suggests a robust mechanism for judicial accountability and performance evaluation.
• The recommendations aim to address the long-standing issues of case backlog and access to justice.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees which are constituted from time to time.
They are of two kinds: Departmental Standing Committees and other Standing Committees.
The Law and Justice Committee examines bills and reports related to the Ministry of Law and Justice.
Judicial pendency is a critical issue in India, with millions of cases awaiting resolution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian judiciary in ensuring speedy justice and effective governance.
Analyze the significance of parliamentary recommendations in shaping judicial reforms.
Evaluate the potential impact of technology (AI) on improving judicial efficiency and transparency.
Examine the balance between judicial independence and accountability in the context of proposed reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a gavel and scales of justice, symbolizing judicial reforms.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Lok Adalats' (People's Courts) in India is an alternative dispute resolution mechanism aimed at speedy and inexpensive justice.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Headwinds; Focus on Inflation Targeting Continues
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and volatile crude oil prices.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to inflation targeting, aiming to bring retail inflation within the target band of 2-6%.
• The central bank highlighted robust domestic demand but warned of potential risks from geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions.
• Experts suggest a cautious approach to support economic growth while managing price stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the RBI responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
It consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a monetary policy regime where the central bank aims to achieve a specific inflation rate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing economic growth and inflation control in the current global scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of the inflation targeting framework in maintaining macroeconomic stability in India.
Evaluate the implications of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
Examine the role of global factors, such as crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions, on India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI emblem with graphs showing economic trends.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: RBI and its functions, Monetary Policy Committee

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Economy and Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were reached on enhancing cooperation in the digital economy, including digital payments and data sharing.
• Maritime security emerged as a crucial area of collaboration, with joint efforts planned to counter piracy and promote freedom of navigation.
• India emphasized its 'Act East' policy, reinforcing its engagement with the Southeast Asian bloc.
• Discussions also covered trade facilitation, climate change, and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East' policy, launched in 2014, is a successor to the 'Look East' policy.
The South China Sea is a major point of contention in maritime security in the region.
Digital payments and cross-border data flows are becoming key areas of economic cooperation.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of India's 'Act East' policy in strengthening its strategic ties with ASEAN nations.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in the digital economy and maritime security.
Analyze how enhanced collaboration with ASEAN contributes to India's broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of regional groupings like ASEAN in promoting multilateralism and regional stability amidst global power shifts.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed at a summit.
articles.trivia: The ASEAN Secretariat is located in Jakarta, Indonesia.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Government Announces New Incentives for Green Ammonia Production
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced additional incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The new package specifically targets the promotion of green ammonia production, a key derivative of green hydrogen.
• Incentives include production-linked subsidies and tax benefits for setting up large-scale green ammonia plants.
• The move aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen and ammonia exports.
• This initiative is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like fertilizers and shipping.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity (electrolysis).
Green ammonia is produced using green hydrogen and nitrogen from the air, with renewable energy powering the process.
India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Green ammonia is a crucial feedstock for fertilizers and can be used as a marine fuel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen and green ammonia for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of government incentives and policies in fostering the growth of the green hydrogen economy in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen and ammonia production and export.
Examine how green hydrogen can contribute to decarbonizing key industrial sectors and achieving net-zero emissions.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit.
• This technology is crucial for reducing the cost of space access and enabling more frequent missions.
• ISRO aims to develop a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle in the future.
• The RLV program is part of India's long-term vision for sustainable and cost-effective space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop all critical technologies for a fully reusable orbital launch vehicle.
The current test focused on autonomous landing capabilities, which are essential for re-entry and recovery.
Reusable launch vehicles significantly reduce the cost per launch by allowing components to be used multiple times.
Other countries and private companies like SpaceX are also developing reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's future space missions and commercial ventures.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV program contributes to reducing space access costs and enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
Examine the broader implications of such advancements for national security and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype descending to a landing strip.
articles.trivia: ISRO launched its first satellite, Aryabhata, in 1975.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive Cyber Security Policy 2024 to Counter Emerging Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Policy 2024.
• The policy aims to strengthen India's cyber resilience against state-sponsored attacks, cyber terrorism, and data breaches.
• Key pillars include critical infrastructure protection, promoting cyber hygiene, skill development, and international cooperation.
• It mandates regular cyber audits for government and critical private sector entities.
• The policy also emphasizes public-private partnerships for threat intelligence sharing and incident response.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: The previous National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for the functioning of a nation, such as power grids, financial systems, and defense networks.
The Personal Data Protection Bill (now Act) is a complementary law for data privacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key provisions of the National Cyber Security Policy 2024 and evaluate its potential effectiveness in safeguarding India's cyberspace.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security framework, including skill gaps and international coordination.
Suggest measures to enhance public-private collaboration in strengthening India's cyber resilience.
articles.imageDescription: Digital shield icon representing cyber security with a map of India in the background.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top countries experiencing cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Cyber Security Policy, Threats and Measures

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report lauded significant progress in integrating vocational education and skill development into mainstream curricula.
• Emphasis was placed on the establishment of 'Skill Hubs' and credit transfer mechanisms for vocational courses.
• Challenges remain in teacher training and infrastructure development, particularly in rural areas.
• The NEP aims to transform India into a knowledge society and global skill hub by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
Vocational education is to be integrated from Class 6 onwards.
The policy aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020, particularly concerning skill development.
Discuss how the integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling can address India's demographic dividend and unemployment issues.
Evaluate the role of teacher training and infrastructure in achieving the holistic goals of NEP 2020.
Examine the potential of NEP 2020 to transform India into a global knowledge and skill hub.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was one of the first to strongly advocate for vocational education in India.

articles.headline: Remembering Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel on His Death Anniversary: A Visionary for United India
articles.explainer:
• Today marks the 74th death anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, one of India's most revered freedom fighters and statesmen.
• Patel is famously known as the 'Iron Man of India' for his pivotal role in integrating over 500 princely states into the Indian Union.
• His administrative acumen and unwavering commitment to national unity laid the foundation for modern India.
• He served as India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.
• Various events were organized across the nation to pay tribute to his legacy and contributions.
gsSubTopics: The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
prelimsPoints: Sardar Patel was born on October 31, 1875, celebrated as National Unity Day (Rashtriya Ekta Diwas).
He played a crucial role in the Bardoli Satyagraha in 1928, earning him the title 'Sardar'.
He was instrumental in the integration of princely states like Junagadh, Hyderabad, and Kashmir.
The Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue, is dedicated to Sardar Patel.
mainsPoints: Discuss Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel's unparalleled contribution to the post-independence consolidation and reorganization of India.
Analyze the challenges faced by Patel in integrating princely states and the strategies he employed to achieve national unity.
Evaluate the relevance of Sardar Patel's vision of a strong and united India in contemporary times.
Examine his administrative and diplomatic skills in shaping the early years of independent India.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, 'Iron Man of India'.
articles.trivia: Sardar Patel was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1991.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves PLI Scheme Expansion for Advanced Chemistry Cell Battery Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet gave its nod to expand the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
• The revised scheme includes higher incentives and broader eligibility criteria to attract more global and domestic players.
• This move aims to boost India's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem and reduce reliance on battery imports.
• The government expects significant investment and job creation in the advanced manufacturing sector.
• ACC batteries are crucial for energy storage solutions, including EVs and grid-scale applications.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was launched to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce imports.
ACC batteries are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy.
India is heavily dependent on imports for lithium-ion cells.
The FAME India scheme promotes the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's EV and renewable energy targets.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for advanced battery production.
Evaluate how this expansion contributes to the 'Make in India' initiative and reduces import dependency.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of promoting indigenous battery manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a battery cell with gears, symbolizing manufacturing and innovation.
articles.trivia: The global demand for lithium-ion batteries is projected to grow exponentially due to the rise of EVs.

articles.headline: Tiger Census 2024 Reveals Stable Population; New Conservation Zones Identified
articles.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census 2024) report was released, indicating a stable tiger population across the country.
• The report highlighted successful conservation efforts but also noted emerging challenges like habitat fragmentation.
• Several new potential tiger conservation zones were identified in central and northeastern India.
• The census employed advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, for more accurate data.
• India continues to be home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India conducts a tiger census every four years.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is an app-based monitoring system used in tiger reserves.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and successes of India's tiger conservation efforts, especially in light of the latest tiger census report.
Analyze the importance of identifying new conservation zones and ensuring corridor connectivity for long-term tiger survival.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological advancements in effective wildlife management.
Examine the socio-economic implications of human-wildlife conflict and strategies for mitigation in tiger habitats.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing conservation.
articles.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals, except during mating season or when a mother is raising her cubs.

articles.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at COP29 Follow-up
articles.explainer:
• At a follow-up meeting to COP29, India announced a substantial financial pledge to the newly established Global South Climate Adaptation Fund.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in building resilience against climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• The move reinforces India's leadership role among developing countries on climate action.
• Discussions also focused on technology transfer and capacity building for climate adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: COP29 was held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
The concept of 'Loss and Damage' refers to the unavoidable impacts of climate change.
The Global South refers to developing countries, primarily located in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
CBDR-RC is a principle under UNFCCC that acknowledges different responsibilities for climate action based on historical emissions and development levels.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role in global climate negotiations and its leadership among the Global South.
Analyze the significance of climate adaptation funds for developing countries and the principle of CBDR-RC.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and ensuring equitable distribution to vulnerable nations.
Examine how India's commitment contributes to achieving global climate goals and fostering international cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing developing countries (Global South) with a climate change overlay.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

articles.headline: E-Governance Initiative 'Jan Seva' Expands to All Districts, Enhancing Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions announced the pan-India rollout of the 'Jan Seva' e-governance platform.
• The platform, which digitizes various government services, is now operational in all districts across the country.
• It aims to provide transparent, efficient, and citizen-centric services, reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Services include application for certificates, utility bill payments, and grievance redressal.
• The expansion is a major step towards achieving 'Maximum Governance, Minimum Government'.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations

Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
articles.source: Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances
prelimsPoints: E-governance refers to the application of information and communication technology (ICT) for delivering government services.
The Digital India program aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Common Service Centres (CSCs) provide various e-governance services in rural and remote areas.
The 'Maximum Governance, Minimum Government' slogan emphasizes efficiency and reduced bureaucracy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives like 'Jan Seva' in enhancing public service delivery and promoting good governance in India.
Analyze the challenges encountered in achieving universal access and digital literacy for e-governance platforms, especially in rural areas.
Evaluate the impact of 'Jan Seva' on reducing corruption and increasing transparency in government functions.
Suggest measures to further strengthen e-governance frameworks and bridge the digital divide in India.
articles.imageDescription: A hand interacting with a holographic interface displaying government services.
articles.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 to lay the foundation for e-governance in India.

articles.headline: Centre Facilitates Dialogue for Resolution of Inter-State Border Disputes in Northeast
articles.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry initiated a fresh round of dialogues between several Northeastern states to resolve long-standing inter-state border disputes.
• The discussions aim to find amicable and lasting solutions, fostering peace and development in the region.
• A special committee of experts has been formed to review historical claims and ground realities.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader strategy to enhance regional cooperation and stability in the Northeast.
• Previous efforts have seen limited success, making this renewed push critical for regional harmony.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Inter-state border disputes in India are primarily historical, stemming from colonial administrative boundaries and state reorganization.
The North Eastern Council (NEC) is a statutory advisory body for the socio-economic development of the Northeastern states.
Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Inter-State Council to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states.
States like Assam, Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Arunachal Pradesh have active border disputes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical and political factors contributing to inter-state border disputes in Northeast India.
Analyze the implications of unresolved border disputes on regional development, security, and ethnic harmony.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various mechanisms, including central intervention and judicial pronouncements, in resolving such disputes.
Suggest a comprehensive approach for achieving lasting peace and cooperation among the Northeastern states regarding their borders.
articles.imageDescription: Map of Northeast India with disputed border lines highlighted.
articles.trivia: The 'Assam Accord' (1985) addressed issues like illegal immigration and border management in Assam.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds EWS Reservation with New Clarifications on 'Creamy Layer' Equivalent
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the constitutional validity of the 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
• The court provided new clarifications regarding the income criteria and potentially introduced a 'creamy layer' equivalent for EWS reservation.
• This ruling aims to ensure that the benefits of EWS reservation reach the truly deserving and prevent misuse.
• The judgment addresses petitions challenging the existing EWS criteria and its implementation.
• It is expected to have significant implications for admissions in educational institutions and government employment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, introduced Articles 15(6) and 16(6), providing for EWS reservation.
The EWS reservation is for those not covered under existing reservations for SCs, STs, and OBCs.
The 'creamy layer' concept was introduced for OBC reservations to exclude affluent individuals from reservation benefits.
The Supreme Court had previously upheld the EWS reservation in 2022 but left room for further clarification on criteria.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and socio-economic implications of the 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale behind introducing a 'creamy layer' equivalent for EWS and its potential impact on social justice.
Evaluate the challenges in defining and implementing criteria for economic backwardness to ensure that reservation benefits reach the most deserving.
Examine the broader debate on reservation policies in India and the need for periodic review and reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a stylized 'EWS' text.
articles.trivia: The Indra Sawhney case (1992), also known as the 'Mandal Commission case', capped reservations at 50%.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Constitutional Amendments, Fundamental Rights, Reservation Policy

articles.headline: New National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024 Launched to Promote Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024.
• The policy emphasizes a 'circular economy' approach, focusing on waste reduction, reuse, and recycling.
• It mandates segregation of waste at source, promotes decentralized composting, and waste-to-energy projects.
• Incentives for private sector participation and technological innovation in waste management are also outlined.
• The policy aims to achieve 100% scientific waste processing in all urban areas by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, are the primary legal framework for waste management in India.
A circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible, extracting the maximum value from them whilst in use.
Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (Urban) focuses on achieving open defecation free (ODF) status and scientific solid waste management.
Waste-to-Energy plants convert non-recyclable waste materials into usable heat, electricity, or fuel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban waste management in India and the need for a comprehensive policy like the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024.
Analyze how the 'circular economy' approach can transform waste management practices and contribute to sustainable urban development.
Evaluate the role of technology, private sector involvement, and citizen participation in achieving 100% scientific waste processing.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental benefits of effective waste management, including job creation and pollution reduction.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing waste segregation bins and recycling symbols in an urban setting.
articles.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste per day.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Tourism Sector, Focus on Spiritual and Eco-Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched a new National Tourism Policy, aiming to revive and strengthen the sector post-pandemic.
• The policy emphasizes promoting India's rich spiritual heritage and diverse eco-tourism destinations.
• It includes provisions for infrastructure development, skill enhancement for tourism professionals, and digital promotion.
• Special focus is given to sustainable tourism practices and community involvement.
• The goal is to make India a top global tourism destination by 2030, contributing significantly to GDP and employment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The tourism sector is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
Swadesh Darshan Scheme aims for integrated development of theme-based tourist circuits.
PRASAD Scheme focuses on pilgrimage rejuvenation and spiritual, heritage augmentation drive.
Eco-tourism emphasizes responsible travel to natural areas that conserves the environment and improves the well-being of local people.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's tourism sector, particularly spiritual and eco-tourism, to drive economic growth and employment.
Analyze the key features of the new National Tourism Policy and its strategies for sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Indian tourism sector, including infrastructure gaps and environmental concerns, and how the new policy addresses them.
Examine the role of digital promotion and community participation in enhancing India's global tourism competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse Indian landscapes, from mountains to temples, representing tourism.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top 20 countries in the world for international tourist arrivals.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Interest for Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is gaining significant international traction.
• Several developing countries are exploring adopting similar frameworks to foster financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
• The G20 forum has recognized DPI as a critical enabler for sustainable development goals.
• India is actively offering technical assistance and sharing its expertise with partner nations.
• This model showcases India's leadership in leveraging technology for inclusive growth and digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that enable essential functions like identity, payments, and data exchange.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents and certificates digitally.
The G20 has emphasized the importance of DPI for accelerating progress on SDGs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for inclusive growth and governance in India.
Analyze how key components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker have contributed to financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
Evaluate the reasons behind the growing global interest in India's DPI model and its implications for India's soft power.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, security, and digital literacy in expanding DPI to developing countries.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other DPI components.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

articles.headline: AYUSH Ministry Promotes Integration of Traditional Medicine in Public Health Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH announced new guidelines to facilitate the integration of traditional Indian medicine systems into mainstream public health programs.
• The initiative aims to leverage Ayurveda, Yoga, Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy (AYUSH) for preventive and promotive healthcare.
• It includes training for AYUSH practitioners in primary healthcare settings and standardized treatment protocols.
• The move is part of a holistic approach to healthcare, combining modern and traditional practices.
• Focus areas include lifestyle diseases, maternal and child health, and geriatric care.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: AYUSH stands for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy.
The Ministry of AYUSH was formed in 2014.
India has a rich heritage of traditional medicine systems.
The National Health Policy 2017 advocates for mainstreaming AYUSH systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of integrating traditional Indian medicine systems (AYUSH) into the mainstream public health system.
Analyze how AYUSH can contribute to preventive and promotive healthcare, especially in addressing lifestyle diseases and rural health needs.
Evaluate the steps taken by the Ministry of AYUSH to standardize practices, ensure quality control, and promote research in traditional medicine.
Examine the concept of 'medical pluralism' and its relevance in achieving universal health coverage in India.
articles.imageDescription: Various symbols of AYUSH systems (Ayurveda leaf, Yoga pose, etc.) converging.
articles.trivia: The first World Ayurveda Congress was held in Pune, India, in 2002.

articles.headline: Parliament Winter Session Concludes with High Productivity Amidst Key Legislation Passage
articles.explainer:
• The Winter Session of Parliament concluded, recording high productivity in terms of legislative business.
• Several crucial bills were passed, including amendments to economic laws and social welfare schemes.
• Debates on pressing national issues, such as economic growth and climate change, also took place.
• The session saw active participation from members across party lines in legislative discussions.
• This marks a positive trend in parliamentary functioning, contrasting with previous sessions often marred by disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Parliament typically has three sessions: Budget Session, Monsoon Session, and Winter Session.
A bill passed by both Houses of Parliament becomes an Act after receiving the President's assent.
The 'Productivity of Parliament' is measured by the percentage of time utilized for legislative and other business.
Question Hour and Zero Hour are important mechanisms for parliamentary oversight.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of parliamentary sessions in a democratic setup, particularly in legislative oversight and policy formulation.
Analyze the factors contributing to the high productivity of the recently concluded Winter Session of Parliament.
Evaluate the challenges to parliamentary functioning in India, such as frequent disruptions and declining quality of debates.
Suggest measures to enhance parliamentary efficiency and ensure effective discharge of its duties as a legislative and deliberative body.
articles.imageDescription: Parliament House of India with a clock showing time, symbolizing productivity.
articles.trivia: The longest session of the Indian Parliament was the first Lok Sabha's Budget Session in 1952, which lasted for 127 days.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Sign New Bilateral Trade Agreement to Boost Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil signed a new bilateral trade agreement aimed at diversifying and expanding their economic partnership.
• The agreement focuses on reducing tariffs on agricultural products, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components.
• It also includes provisions for greater cooperation in services, investment, and intellectual property rights.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening BRICS cooperation and South-South collaboration.
• The deal is expected to significantly increase bilateral trade volume and foster closer strategic ties.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Brazil is India's largest trading partner in Latin America.
BRICS is an acronym for the grouping of the world's leading emerging economies: Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa.
South-South cooperation refers to the exchange of resources, technology, and knowledge between developing countries.
Mercosur is a South American trade bloc, and Brazil is a founding member.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India-Brazil bilateral relations, particularly in the context of South-South cooperation and BRICS.
Analyze the key provisions of the new bilateral trade agreement and its potential impact on diversifying economic ties between the two nations.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for increasing trade and investment flows between India and Brazil.
Examine how enhanced cooperation with Latin American countries like Brazil can contribute to India's global economic and geopolitical objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: Brazil is the world's largest producer of coffee.

highlights: Parliamentary Panel recommends sweeping judicial reforms for efficiency and accountability, focusing on specialized courts and digital integration.
RBI maintains repo rate amidst global uncertainties, reiterating commitment to inflation targeting while supporting robust domestic demand.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with significant agreements on digital economy cooperation and strengthening maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
National Green Hydrogen Mission expands with new incentives for Green Ammonia production, aiming to make India a global export hub.
ISRO successfully tests its next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, a major step towards cost-effective and sustainable space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity (electrolysis).
3. Green ammonia is a derivative of green hydrogen primarily used in fertilizer production and as a marine fuel.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen and its derivatives like green ammonia. Green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis powered by renewable energy, and green ammonia is a key application for decarbonizing sectors like fertilizers and shipping.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the RBI responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India. It consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

prelimsMocks.question:
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is famously known for his pivotal role in:
1. Leading the Salt Satyagraha.
2. Integrating over 500 princely states into the Indian Union.
3. Drafting the Constitution of India.
4. Initiating the Green Revolution in India.
Select the correct statement:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is most famously known as the 'Iron Man of India' for his crucial role in integrating over 500 princely states into the Indian Union after independence. Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt Satyagraha. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chief architect of the Indian Constitution. The Green Revolution was initiated much later, primarily by M.S. Swaminathan in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
2. UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is an instant real-time payment system.
3. DigiLocker is a platform for digital issuance and verification of documents.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements accurately describe components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure. Aadhaar provides unique identity, UPI enables instant payments, and DigiLocker facilitates digital document management.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the AYUSH systems of medicine?
prelimsMocks.explanation: AYUSH is an acronym for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy. Allopathy refers to conventional Western medicine and is not part of the AYUSH systems.

mainsMocks.question: The Indian judiciary faces significant challenges in ensuring speedy justice and accessibility. Critically analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice for judicial reforms, and discuss how these measures can enhance efficiency and accountability within the system.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the issue of judicial pendency. Detail the key recommendations (specialized courts, tech integration, appointments, accountability). Analyze potential benefits (speedy justice, transparency, reduced backlog) and challenges (implementation, resource allocation, judicial independence). Conclude with a balanced perspective on balancing reform with constitutional principles.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Act East' policy has been instrumental in strengthening its strategic ties with Southeast Asian nations. Discuss the significance of the recent India-ASEAN Summit, highlighting the emerging areas of cooperation such as the digital economy and maritime security, and evaluate their implications for India's Indo-Pacific strategy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the 'Act East' policy's evolution and objectives. Explain the importance of ASEAN for India. Detail the key outcomes of the summit, focusing on digital economy (payments, data) and maritime security (piracy, freedom of navigation). Link these to India's broader Indo-Pacific vision and its role as a net security provider and economic partner. Conclude on the strategic convergence and future prospects.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global leader in green energy. Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen and green ammonia for India's energy security and climate goals. Analyze the role of government incentives and technological advancements in fostering the growth of this sector and achieving net-zero emissions.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Define green hydrogen and green ammonia. Explain their strategic importance for energy independence, decarbonization of hard-to-abate sectors, and climate targets. Detail government incentives (e.g., PLI scheme) and technological advancements (electrolysis, storage). Discuss challenges (cost, infrastructure, R&D) and opportunities (job creation, exports). Conclude on India's potential to achieve its climate commitments and become a green energy hub.

quickBytes.text: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act introduced 10% EWS reservation.
Project Tiger was launched in India in 1973 for tiger conservation.
ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members.
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was India's first Deputy Prime Minister.
ASEAN comprises ten Southeast Asian countries.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
Green hydrogen production uses renewable energy for electrolysis.
CERT-In is India's national agency for cyber security incidents.
UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

GS1.headline: Remembering Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel on His Death Anniversary: A Visionary for United India
GS1.explainer:
• Today marks the 74th death anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, one of India's most revered freedom fighters and statesmen.
• Patel is famously known as the 'Iron Man of India' for his pivotal role in integrating over 500 princely states into the Indian Union.
• His administrative acumen and unwavering commitment to national unity laid the foundation for modern India.
• He served as India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.
• Various events were organized across the nation to pay tribute to his legacy and contributions.
gsSubTopics: The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
prelimsPoints: Sardar Patel was born on October 31, 1875, celebrated as National Unity Day (Rashtriya Ekta Diwas).
He played a crucial role in the Bardoli Satyagraha in 1928, earning him the title 'Sardar'.
He was instrumental in the integration of princely states like Junagadh, Hyderabad, and Kashmir.
The Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue, is dedicated to Sardar Patel.
mainsPoints: Discuss Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel's unparalleled contribution to the post-independence consolidation and reorganization of India.
Analyze the challenges faced by Patel in integrating princely states and the strategies he employed to achieve national unity.
Evaluate the relevance of Sardar Patel's vision of a strong and united India in contemporary times.
Examine his administrative and diplomatic skills in shaping the early years of independent India.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, 'Iron Man of India'.
GS1.trivia: Sardar Patel was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1991.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Judicial Reforms: Focus on Efficiency and Accountability
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted its report, urging comprehensive reforms in the Indian judiciary.
• Key recommendations include setting up specialized commercial courts, increasing judicial appointments, and improving infrastructure.
• The report emphasizes leveraging AI and digital tools for case management and reducing pendency.
• It also suggests a robust mechanism for judicial accountability and performance evaluation.
• The recommendations aim to address the long-standing issues of case backlog and access to justice.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees are permanent and regular committees which are constituted from time to time.
They are of two kinds: Departmental Standing Committees and other Standing Committees.
The Law and Justice Committee examines bills and reports related to the Ministry of Law and Justice.
Judicial pendency is a critical issue in India, with millions of cases awaiting resolution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian judiciary in ensuring speedy justice and effective governance.
Analyze the significance of parliamentary recommendations in shaping judicial reforms.
Evaluate the potential impact of technology (AI) on improving judicial efficiency and transparency.
Examine the balance between judicial independence and accountability in the context of proposed reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a gavel and scales of justice, symbolizing judicial reforms.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Lok Adalats' (People's Courts) in India is an alternative dispute resolution mechanism aimed at speedy and inexpensive justice.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Economy and Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were reached on enhancing cooperation in the digital economy, including digital payments and data sharing.
• Maritime security emerged as a crucial area of collaboration, with joint efforts planned to counter piracy and promote freedom of navigation.
• India emphasized its 'Act East' policy, reinforcing its engagement with the Southeast Asian bloc.
• Discussions also covered trade facilitation, climate change, and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) is a regional intergovernmental organization comprising ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East' policy, launched in 2014, is a successor to the 'Look East' policy.
The South China Sea is a major point of contention in maritime security in the region.
Digital payments and cross-border data flows are becoming key areas of economic cooperation.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of India's 'Act East' policy in strengthening its strategic ties with ASEAN nations.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in the digital economy and maritime security.
Analyze how enhanced collaboration with ASEAN contributes to India's broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of regional groupings like ASEAN in promoting multilateralism and regional stability amidst global power shifts.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed at a summit.
GS2.trivia: The ASEAN Secretariat is located in Jakarta, Indonesia.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report lauded significant progress in integrating vocational education and skill development into mainstream curricula.
• Emphasis was placed on the establishment of 'Skill Hubs' and credit transfer mechanisms for vocational courses.
• Challenges remain in teacher training and infrastructure development, particularly in rural areas.
• The NEP aims to transform India into a knowledge society and global skill hub by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
Vocational education is to be integrated from Class 6 onwards.
The policy aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020, particularly concerning skill development.
Discuss how the integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling can address India's demographic dividend and unemployment issues.
Evaluate the role of teacher training and infrastructure in achieving the holistic goals of NEP 2020.
Examine the potential of NEP 2020 to transform India into a global knowledge and skill hub.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities, symbolizing skill development.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was one of the first to strongly advocate for vocational education in India.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at COP29 Follow-up
GS2.explainer:
• At a follow-up meeting to COP29, India announced a substantial financial pledge to the newly established Global South Climate Adaptation Fund.
• The fund aims to support developing nations in building resilience against climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• The move reinforces India's leadership role among developing countries on climate action.
• Discussions also focused on technology transfer and capacity building for climate adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: COP29 was held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
The concept of 'Loss and Damage' refers to the unavoidable impacts of climate change.
The Global South refers to developing countries, primarily located in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
CBDR-RC is a principle under UNFCCC that acknowledges different responsibilities for climate action based on historical emissions and development levels.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role in global climate negotiations and its leadership among the Global South.
Analyze the significance of climate adaptation funds for developing countries and the principle of CBDR-RC.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and ensuring equitable distribution to vulnerable nations.
Examine how India's commitment contributes to achieving global climate goals and fostering international cooperation.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing developing countries (Global South) with a climate change overlay.
GS2.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS2.headline: E-Governance Initiative 'Jan Seva' Expands to All Districts, Enhancing Service Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions announced the pan-India rollout of the 'Jan Seva' e-governance platform.
• The platform, which digitizes various government services, is now operational in all districts across the country.
• It aims to provide transparent, efficient, and citizen-centric services, reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Services include application for certificates, utility bill payments, and grievance redressal.
• The expansion is a major step towards achieving 'Maximum Governance, Minimum Government'.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations

Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
GS2.source: Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances
prelimsPoints: E-governance refers to the application of information and communication technology (ICT) for delivering government services.
The Digital India program aims to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Common Service Centres (CSCs) provide various e-governance services in rural and remote areas.
The 'Maximum Governance, Minimum Government' slogan emphasizes efficiency and reduced bureaucracy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives like 'Jan Seva' in enhancing public service delivery and promoting good governance in India.
Analyze the challenges encountered in achieving universal access and digital literacy for e-governance platforms, especially in rural areas.
Evaluate the impact of 'Jan Seva' on reducing corruption and increasing transparency in government functions.
Suggest measures to further strengthen e-governance frameworks and bridge the digital divide in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand interacting with a holographic interface displaying government services.
GS2.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 to lay the foundation for e-governance in India.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds EWS Reservation with New Clarifications on 'Creamy Layer' Equivalent
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the constitutional validity of the 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
• The court provided new clarifications regarding the income criteria and potentially introduced a 'creamy layer' equivalent for EWS reservation.
• This ruling aims to ensure that the benefits of EWS reservation reach the truly deserving and prevent misuse.
• The judgment addresses petitions challenging the existing EWS criteria and its implementation.
• It is expected to have significant implications for admissions in educational institutions and government employment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: The 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, introduced Articles 15(6) and 16(6), providing for EWS reservation.
The EWS reservation is for those not covered under existing reservations for SCs, STs, and OBCs.
The 'creamy layer' concept was introduced for OBC reservations to exclude affluent individuals from reservation benefits.
The Supreme Court had previously upheld the EWS reservation in 2022 but left room for further clarification on criteria.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and socio-economic implications of the 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).
Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale behind introducing a 'creamy layer' equivalent for EWS and its potential impact on social justice.
Evaluate the challenges in defining and implementing criteria for economic backwardness to ensure that reservation benefits reach the most deserving.
Examine the broader debate on reservation policies in India and the need for periodic review and reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a stylized 'EWS' text.
GS2.trivia: The Indra Sawhney case (1992), also known as the 'Mandal Commission case', capped reservations at 50%.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Constitutional Amendments, Fundamental Rights, Reservation Policy

GS2.headline: AYUSH Ministry Promotes Integration of Traditional Medicine in Public Health Systems
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH announced new guidelines to facilitate the integration of traditional Indian medicine systems into mainstream public health programs.
• The initiative aims to leverage Ayurveda, Yoga, Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy (AYUSH) for preventive and promotive healthcare.
• It includes training for AYUSH practitioners in primary healthcare settings and standardized treatment protocols.
• The move is part of a holistic approach to healthcare, combining modern and traditional practices.
• Focus areas include lifestyle diseases, maternal and child health, and geriatric care.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: AYUSH stands for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy.
The Ministry of AYUSH was formed in 2014.
India has a rich heritage of traditional medicine systems.
The National Health Policy 2017 advocates for mainstreaming AYUSH systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of integrating traditional Indian medicine systems (AYUSH) into the mainstream public health system.
Analyze how AYUSH can contribute to preventive and promotive healthcare, especially in addressing lifestyle diseases and rural health needs.
Evaluate the steps taken by the Ministry of AYUSH to standardize practices, ensure quality control, and promote research in traditional medicine.
Examine the concept of 'medical pluralism' and its relevance in achieving universal health coverage in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Various symbols of AYUSH systems (Ayurveda leaf, Yoga pose, etc.) converging.
GS2.trivia: The first World Ayurveda Congress was held in Pune, India, in 2002.

GS2.headline: Parliament Winter Session Concludes with High Productivity Amidst Key Legislation Passage
GS2.explainer:
• The Winter Session of Parliament concluded, recording high productivity in terms of legislative business.
• Several crucial bills were passed, including amendments to economic laws and social welfare schemes.
• Debates on pressing national issues, such as economic growth and climate change, also took place.
• The session saw active participation from members across party lines in legislative discussions.
• This marks a positive trend in parliamentary functioning, contrasting with previous sessions often marred by disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Parliament typically has three sessions: Budget Session, Monsoon Session, and Winter Session.
A bill passed by both Houses of Parliament becomes an Act after receiving the President's assent.
The 'Productivity of Parliament' is measured by the percentage of time utilized for legislative and other business.
Question Hour and Zero Hour are important mechanisms for parliamentary oversight.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of parliamentary sessions in a democratic setup, particularly in legislative oversight and policy formulation.
Analyze the factors contributing to the high productivity of the recently concluded Winter Session of Parliament.
Evaluate the challenges to parliamentary functioning in India, such as frequent disruptions and declining quality of debates.
Suggest measures to enhance parliamentary efficiency and ensure effective discharge of its duties as a legislative and deliberative body.
GS2.imageDescription: Parliament House of India with a clock showing time, symbolizing productivity.
GS2.trivia: The longest session of the Indian Parliament was the first Lok Sabha's Budget Session in 1952, which lasted for 127 days.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Sign New Bilateral Trade Agreement to Boost Economic Ties
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil signed a new bilateral trade agreement aimed at diversifying and expanding their economic partnership.
• The agreement focuses on reducing tariffs on agricultural products, pharmaceuticals, and automotive components.
• It also includes provisions for greater cooperation in services, investment, and intellectual property rights.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening BRICS cooperation and South-South collaboration.
• The deal is expected to significantly increase bilateral trade volume and foster closer strategic ties.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Brazil is India's largest trading partner in Latin America.
BRICS is an acronym for the grouping of the world's leading emerging economies: Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa.
South-South cooperation refers to the exchange of resources, technology, and knowledge between developing countries.
Mercosur is a South American trade bloc, and Brazil is a founding member.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India-Brazil bilateral relations, particularly in the context of South-South cooperation and BRICS.
Analyze the key provisions of the new bilateral trade agreement and its potential impact on diversifying economic ties between the two nations.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for increasing trade and investment flows between India and Brazil.
Examine how enhanced cooperation with Latin American countries like Brazil can contribute to India's global economic and geopolitical objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: Brazil is the world's largest producer of coffee.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Headwinds; Focus on Inflation Targeting Continues
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• This decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and volatile crude oil prices.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to inflation targeting, aiming to bring retail inflation within the target band of 2-6%.
• The central bank highlighted robust domestic demand but warned of potential risks from geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions.
• Experts suggest a cautious approach to support economic growth while managing price stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the RBI responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
It consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a monetary policy regime where the central bank aims to achieve a specific inflation rate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing economic growth and inflation control in the current global scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of the inflation targeting framework in maintaining macroeconomic stability in India.
Evaluate the implications of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
Examine the role of global factors, such as crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions, on India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI emblem with graphs showing economic trends.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: RBI and its functions, Monetary Policy Committee

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Government Announces New Incentives for Green Ammonia Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced additional incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The new package specifically targets the promotion of green ammonia production, a key derivative of green hydrogen.
• Incentives include production-linked subsidies and tax benefits for setting up large-scale green ammonia plants.
• The move aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen and ammonia exports.
• This initiative is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like fertilizers and shipping.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity (electrolysis).
Green ammonia is produced using green hydrogen and nitrogen from the air, with renewable energy powering the process.
India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Green ammonia is a crucial feedstock for fertilizers and can be used as a marine fuel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen and green ammonia for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of government incentives and policies in fostering the growth of the green hydrogen economy in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen and ammonia production and export.
Examine how green hydrogen can contribute to decarbonizing key industrial sectors and achieving net-zero emissions.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit.
• This technology is crucial for reducing the cost of space access and enabling more frequent missions.
• ISRO aims to develop a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle in the future.
• The RLV program is part of India's long-term vision for sustainable and cost-effective space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop all critical technologies for a fully reusable orbital launch vehicle.
The current test focused on autonomous landing capabilities, which are essential for re-entry and recovery.
Reusable launch vehicles significantly reduce the cost per launch by allowing components to be used multiple times.
Other countries and private companies like SpaceX are also developing reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's future space missions and commercial ventures.
Analyze how ISRO's RLV program contributes to reducing space access costs and enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
Examine the broader implications of such advancements for national security and scientific research.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype descending to a landing strip.
GS3.trivia: ISRO launched its first satellite, Aryabhata, in 1975.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive Cyber Security Policy 2024 to Counter Emerging Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Policy 2024.
• The policy aims to strengthen India's cyber resilience against state-sponsored attacks, cyber terrorism, and data breaches.
• Key pillars include critical infrastructure protection, promoting cyber hygiene, skill development, and international cooperation.
• It mandates regular cyber audits for government and critical private sector entities.
• The policy also emphasizes public-private partnerships for threat intelligence sharing and incident response.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: The previous National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for the functioning of a nation, such as power grids, financial systems, and defense networks.
The Personal Data Protection Bill (now Act) is a complementary law for data privacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key provisions of the National Cyber Security Policy 2024 and evaluate its potential effectiveness in safeguarding India's cyberspace.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security framework, including skill gaps and international coordination.
Suggest measures to enhance public-private collaboration in strengthening India's cyber resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital shield icon representing cyber security with a map of India in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top countries experiencing cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Cyber Security Policy, Threats and Measures

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves PLI Scheme Expansion for Advanced Chemistry Cell Battery Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet gave its nod to expand the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
• The revised scheme includes higher incentives and broader eligibility criteria to attract more global and domestic players.
• This move aims to boost India's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem and reduce reliance on battery imports.
• The government expects significant investment and job creation in the advanced manufacturing sector.
