• ACC batteries are crucial for energy storage solutions, including EVs and grid-scale applications.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was launched to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce imports.
ACC batteries are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy.
India is heavily dependent on imports for lithium-ion cells.
The FAME India scheme promotes the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's EV and renewable energy targets.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for advanced battery production.
Evaluate how this expansion contributes to the 'Make in India' initiative and reduces import dependency.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of promoting indigenous battery manufacturing.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a battery cell with gears, symbolizing manufacturing and innovation.
GS3.trivia: The global demand for lithium-ion batteries is projected to grow exponentially due to the rise of EVs.

GS3.headline: Tiger Census 2024 Reveals Stable Population; New Conservation Zones Identified
GS3.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census 2024) report was released, indicating a stable tiger population across the country.
• The report highlighted successful conservation efforts but also noted emerging challenges like habitat fragmentation.
• Several new potential tiger conservation zones were identified in central and northeastern India.
• The census employed advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, for more accurate data.
• India continues to be home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India conducts a tiger census every four years.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is an app-based monitoring system used in tiger reserves.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and successes of India's tiger conservation efforts, especially in light of the latest tiger census report.
Analyze the importance of identifying new conservation zones and ensuring corridor connectivity for long-term tiger survival.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological advancements in effective wildlife management.
Examine the socio-economic implications of human-wildlife conflict and strategies for mitigation in tiger habitats.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing conservation.
GS3.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals, except during mating season or when a mother is raising her cubs.

GS3.headline: Centre Facilitates Dialogue for Resolution of Inter-State Border Disputes in Northeast
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry initiated a fresh round of dialogues between several Northeastern states to resolve long-standing inter-state border disputes.
• The discussions aim to find amicable and lasting solutions, fostering peace and development in the region.
• A special committee of experts has been formed to review historical claims and ground realities.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader strategy to enhance regional cooperation and stability in the Northeast.
• Previous efforts have seen limited success, making this renewed push critical for regional harmony.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Inter-state border disputes in India are primarily historical, stemming from colonial administrative boundaries and state reorganization.
The North Eastern Council (NEC) is a statutory advisory body for the socio-economic development of the Northeastern states.
Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Inter-State Council to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states.
States like Assam, Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Arunachal Pradesh have active border disputes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical and political factors contributing to inter-state border disputes in Northeast India.
Analyze the implications of unresolved border disputes on regional development, security, and ethnic harmony.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various mechanisms, including central intervention and judicial pronouncements, in resolving such disputes.
Suggest a comprehensive approach for achieving lasting peace and cooperation among the Northeastern states regarding their borders.
GS3.imageDescription: Map of Northeast India with disputed border lines highlighted.
GS3.trivia: The 'Assam Accord' (1985) addressed issues like illegal immigration and border management in Assam.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

GS3.headline: New National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024 Launched to Promote Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024.
• The policy emphasizes a 'circular economy' approach, focusing on waste reduction, reuse, and recycling.
• It mandates segregation of waste at source, promotes decentralized composting, and waste-to-energy projects.
• Incentives for private sector participation and technological innovation in waste management are also outlined.
• The policy aims to achieve 100% scientific waste processing in all urban areas by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, are the primary legal framework for waste management in India.
A circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible, extracting the maximum value from them whilst in use.
Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (Urban) focuses on achieving open defecation free (ODF) status and scientific solid waste management.
Waste-to-Energy plants convert non-recyclable waste materials into usable heat, electricity, or fuel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban waste management in India and the need for a comprehensive policy like the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2024.
Analyze how the 'circular economy' approach can transform waste management practices and contribute to sustainable urban development.
Evaluate the role of technology, private sector involvement, and citizen participation in achieving 100% scientific waste processing.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental benefits of effective waste management, including job creation and pollution reduction.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic showing waste segregation bins and recycling symbols in an urban setting.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste per day.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Tourism Sector, Focus on Spiritual and Eco-Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched a new National Tourism Policy, aiming to revive and strengthen the sector post-pandemic.
• The policy emphasizes promoting India's rich spiritual heritage and diverse eco-tourism destinations.
• It includes provisions for infrastructure development, skill enhancement for tourism professionals, and digital promotion.
• Special focus is given to sustainable tourism practices and community involvement.
• The goal is to make India a top global tourism destination by 2030, contributing significantly to GDP and employment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The tourism sector is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
Swadesh Darshan Scheme aims for integrated development of theme-based tourist circuits.
PRASAD Scheme focuses on pilgrimage rejuvenation and spiritual, heritage augmentation drive.
Eco-tourism emphasizes responsible travel to natural areas that conserves the environment and improves the well-being of local people.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's tourism sector, particularly spiritual and eco-tourism, to drive economic growth and employment.
Analyze the key features of the new National Tourism Policy and its strategies for sustainable and inclusive tourism development.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Indian tourism sector, including infrastructure gaps and environmental concerns, and how the new policy addresses them.
Examine the role of digital promotion and community participation in enhancing India's global tourism competitiveness.
GS3.imageDescription: Diverse Indian landscapes, from mountains to temples, representing tourism.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top 20 countries in the world for international tourist arrivals.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Interest for Inclusive Growth
GS3.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is gaining significant international traction.
• Several developing countries are exploring adopting similar frameworks to foster financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
• The G20 forum has recognized DPI as a critical enabler for sustainable development goals.
• India is actively offering technical assistance and sharing its expertise with partner nations.
• This model showcases India's leadership in leveraging technology for inclusive growth and digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that enable essential functions like identity, payments, and data exchange.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents and certificates digitally.
The G20 has emphasized the importance of DPI for accelerating progress on SDGs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for inclusive growth and governance in India.
Analyze how key components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker have contributed to financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
Evaluate the reasons behind the growing global interest in India's DPI model and its implications for India's soft power.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, security, and digital literacy in expanding DPI to developing countries.
GS3.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other DPI components.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

sources: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
